summary: Stellar Let It Grow and epic Space>Other One; solid second set
blurb: The show delivers a soaring "Let It Grow" with Phil’s killer bass and Brent’s lush keyboards, plus an explosive "Space > The Other One" jam. The second set builds to a powerful "Terrapin Station" climax, though the Drums‑>Space stretch feels a bit long. Overall a high‑energy performance captured well on both soundboard and audience tapes.
review: September 18, 1983 at the Nevada County Fairgrounds finds the Dead in top form. Jerry Garcia rides the wave of the night, laying down soaring leads on "Let It Grow" and guiding the second‑set journey through a blistering "Space > The Other One" where Brent Mydland slips in a cheeky James Bond quote. Phil Lesh’s low‑booming, walking bass underpins the set with crystal‑clear definition, especially on the title track "Terrapin Station" and the driving "Jack Straw" opener. Bob Weir’s rhythm work shines on "Friend Of The Devil", while Brent fills every sonic nook, adding texture from the first song to the closing encore. Though the Drums‑>Space segment drags for a few minutes, the overall vibe is high‑octane, captured beautifully by the recommended soundboard tape and an excellent audience recording that highlights a standout "Liberty" performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are strongly positive, praising key moments like Let It Grow, Space > The Other One and Terrapin Station; one mixed review notes only a long Drums‑>Space stretch. No consistent negative comments appear, just one mixed sentiment, so a solid positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Good‑sound soundboard capture with clear mix, balanced levels and the most complete representation of the standout songs.

key_highlights: "Let It Grow" showcases a killer Phil bass line and Brent’s textural keyboards.
The "Space > The Other One" jam is epic, highlighted by a James Bond quote on keys.
Second‑set peaks with a powerful "Terrapin Station" despite a lengthy Drums‑>Space stretch.
Audience tape captures the vibrant energy of the night, especially the standout "Liberty" performance.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring leads in the second set and feels the music deeply, though some note a caustic tone later in the tour.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a killer, low‑booming bass line on Let It Grow and is crystal‑clear throughout the mix.
band_performance.Bob: Turns bonkers on Friend Of The Devil and supplies solid rhythm work across the set.
band_performance.Brent: Fills all the spaces with lush textures; shines especially during The Other One jam with a memorable keyboard quote.
